name: [BUG-DB-002] TC-DB-002 — period is case-sensitive (period=WEEK → 400, no documentation)
description:
**[BUG-DB-002]**

The `period` parameter is strictly case-sensitive:
- `period=week` → 200 OK
- `period=WEEK` → 400 Bad Request
- `period=Week` → 400 Bad Request

**Expected:** The API should either accept case-insensitive values and normalise them, OR document explicitly that values are case-sensitive.

**Additionally:** The 400 error message omits `today` and `last-3-month` from the valid values list, leaving developers guessing.

**Impact:** Developers who naturally capitalise query params or use uppercase constants will get unexpected 400 errors with no useful guidance.
descriptionHtml:
[BUG-DB-002]

The period parameter is strictly case-sensitive:

  • period=week → 200 OK
  • period=WEEK → 400 Bad Request
  • period=Week → 400 Bad Request

Expected: The API should either accept case-insensitive values and normalise them, OR document explicitly that values are case-sensitive.

Additionally: The 400 error message omits today and last-3-month from the valid values list, leaving developers guessing.

Impact: Developers who naturally capitalise query params or use uppercase constants will get unexpected 400 errors with no useful guidance.
statusMessage:
Error: period=week must return 200

expect(received).toBe(expected) // Object.is equality

Expected: 200
Received: 400
